metadata.title:
BIG PARADE OF
/ UNION WORKERS.

Organized Labor Celebrates Its HOLIDAY
!. by Marching in Uniform
K. Array.

pages.raw_text:
They carried canes The brewery
workers appeared with dark trousers,
light blue shirts, light belts and black
for ornament. The red shirts and
black caps appeared thoroughly en-
blematical Of the horseshoers. as did
the white suits, aprons and caps of
the bakers and confectioners. The
boilermakers and the iron workers
wore blue denim overalls and blouses
and white caps. The blacksmiths were
uniformed in black, while around
their waists were tied heavy leather
aprons.


The speakers of the day. John r.
Sovereign, Fred N. Foss and c. A.
Foster, rode in a decorated carriage,
followed by another containing the
women who are delegates to the
trades council.


One Of the typical floats was that
of the boilermakers. A group of men
were engaged in driving rivets. Three
young ladies also appeared on this
float The blacksmiths' float repre-
scented a shop in full action. Upon
the transfers float was donkey
cart. The members of the union
turned out in great number driving
their wagons.


Immediately after the parade dis
banded the union men, accompanied
by their families, set out for Nata-
forum Park, where the other exer-
cises of the day were held.





hundreds Of hungry people was a
sumptuous barbecued repast, provid-
ed by the members of the colored
Baptist church.


Dancing was commenced in the pa
vilion at once. and it will be con-
tinued this evening.
